description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone block with rounded edges and a coarse, granular texture. The surface is pitted and uneven, with visible inclusions of small stones and aggregate, suggesting it is made from a natural or minimally processed stone material. On one of the broader faces, there is a single, circular hole that appears to have been intentionally drilled or worn through, possibly for mounting or lifting purposes. The coloration is a consistent earthy brown with some lighter and darker speckles throughout, indicating weathering and exposure to the elements. One of the narrow sides features a faint, hand-written marking or code in white, likely for identification or cataloging. The block is solid and heavy, suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment such as a sidewalk, park, or street.
description: A coarse, rectangular stone block with rounded edges, a drilled hole on one face, and a weathered, speckled brown surface. One side has a faint white marking.
description_view_0: The broad face of the stone block is visible, featuring a prominent circular hole near the upper center and a rough, pitted surface.
description_view_1: A narrow side view showing the block's thickness and rounded edges, with no visible holes or markings.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is shown, also with a circular hole but with slightly different surface pitting and coloration.
description_view_3: The other narrow side is visible, displaying a faint white marking or code and the same rough, granular texture.
attribute_installation_method: set directly on ground or pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface is pitted and uneven, with some discoloration and minor chipping, indicating moderate weathering from outdoor exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A faint white marking or code is visible on one narrow side.
